headline: Accenture’s Full Copilot Rollout Tests AI Productivity At Enterprise Scale
summary: Microsoft is rolling out its Copilot 365 AI assistant to all 743,000 Accenture (NYSE:ACN) employees. The agreement is currently described as the largest enterprise deployment of the Copilot 365 platform. The rollout positions Accenture as an early, large scale corporate adopter of AI tools across its global workforce. For investors watching enterprise IT and consulting, this move highlights how AI tools are starting to sit at the core of day to day work. Accenture operates across...

headline: Salesforce Lawsuit Against Microsoft Puts AI Workflow Ambitions In Focus
summary: Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) and Slack have filed an antitrust lawsuit in the UK against Microsoft, challenging the bundling of Teams with other Microsoft products. The case follows earlier complaints to European authorities and targets how collaboration tools are packaged and sold to enterprise customers. The filing comes as Salesforce promotes its Agentforce platform and Google Cloud integrations for AI driven workflows across large organizations. For investors watching enterprise software, this...

headline: Microsoft Reset With OpenAI Puts Azure AI Moat Under Investor Focus
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have agreed to end Azure cloud exclusivity for OpenAI models and remove revenue sharing between the companies. The revised agreement allows OpenAI to work with other major cloud providers, while Microsoft keeps a multi year IP license and its equity stake. This shift comes as regulators increase scrutiny of Big Tech AI partnerships and as OpenAI weighs a potential IPO. For investors watching NasdaqGS:MSFT, this reset in the OpenAI relationship touches the core of...

headline: Fed News and Big Tech Earnings Collide This Week. The Mag 7 Matter Most.
summary: Wall Street is getting ready for one crazy Wednesday: A Federal Reserve meeting, a Senate committee vote on a new Fed chairman, and earnings reports from four Mag Seven companies after the closing bell.  The Cboe Volatility Index, also known as Wall Street’s fear gauge, has tumbled from above 30 in late March to a more reasonable level of 18.  Adding to the stakes: Never before have powerhouses  Alphabet Microsoft Amazon  and  Meta Platforms  all posted earnings on the same day.

headline: Microsoft faces UK antitrust challenge from Slack over Teams integration
summary: Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), through its messaging platform Slack, has launched legal action against Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) in London’s High Court, accusing the tech giant of anticompetitive conduct linked to its Teams software. The claim, filed on April 23 by Slack Technologies LLC and associated entities, alleges that Microsoft restricted competition by integrating Teams with its broader software offerings.

content.title: Microsoft faces UK antitrust challenge from Slack over Teams integration
content.summary: Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), through its messaging platform Slack, has launched legal action against Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) in London’s High Court, accusing the tech giant of anticompetitive conduct linked to its Teams software. The claim, filed on April 23 by Slack Technologies LLC and associated entities, alleges that Microsoft restricted competition by integrating Teams with its broader software offerings.

headline: OpenAI trial pitting Elon Musk against Sam Altman kicks off
summary: A trial that could help shape the future of artificial intelligence begins on Tuesday, with billionaires Elon Musk and Sam Altman at odds over the evolution of ChatGPT maker OpenAI from a nonprofit to a ‌profit-seeking juggernaut worth hundreds of billions of dollars.  Opening statements in Musk's civil lawsuit against OpenAI and Altman will take place in ‌the Oakland, California, federal court, following the selection on Monday of nine jurors.  Musk claims that Altman and Greg Brockman, respectively OpenAI's chief executive and president, betrayed him and the public ​by abandoning the company's mission to be a benevolent steward of AI for the benefit of humanity, and turning it into a "wealth machine" for themselves and investors.

content.title: OpenAI trial pitting Elon Musk against Sam Altman kicks off
content.summary: A trial that could help shape the future of artificial intelligence begins on Tuesday, with billionaires Elon Musk and Sam Altman at odds over the evolution of ChatGPT maker OpenAI from a nonprofit to a ‌profit-seeking juggernaut worth hundreds of billions of dollars.  Opening statements in Musk's civil lawsuit against OpenAI and Altman will take place in ‌the Oakland, California, federal court, following the selection on Monday of nine jurors.  Musk claims that Altman and Greg Brockman, respectively OpenAI's chief executive and president, betrayed him and the public ​by abandoning the company's mission to be a benevolent steward of AI for the benefit of humanity, and turning it into a "wealth machine" for themselves and investors.

thumbnail.caption: Jury selection in Musk's OpenAI lawsuit, in Oakland

headline: Big Tech investors to gauge payoff as AI spending set to hit $600 billion
summary: Big Tech has spent hundreds of billions of dollars over three years to power the artificial intelligence boom.  Quarterly results from Alphabet, Microsoft, ‌Meta and Amazon - all due on Wednesday - will gauge if the sky-high spending on AI has driven enough growth in cloud ‌computing and advertising to justify the cost.  Amazon and Instagram-parent Meta have announced job cuts affecting thousands of workers, while Microsoft has come up with its first employee buyout program in more than five decades.

content.title: Big Tech investors to gauge payoff as AI spending set to hit $600 billion

Amazon and Instagram-parent Meta have announced job cuts affecting thousands of workers, while Microsoft has come up with its first employee buyout program in more than five decades.

headline: Bitcoin, XRP Prices Fall. What’s Holding Cryptos Back.
summary: Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies were falling early Tuesday as the stock market’s tech-driven rally looked set to hit pause.  Bitcoin  was trading at $76,637 early in the day, down 1.3% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.  Nasdaq futures  slipped 0.4% ahead of the open after the index, and the S&P 500 eked out record closing highs.

content.title: Bitcoin, XRP Prices Fall. What’s Holding Cryptos Back.
content.summary: Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies were falling early Tuesday as the stock market’s tech-driven rally looked set to hit pause.  Bitcoin  was trading at $76,637 early in the day, down 1.3% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.  Nasdaq futures  slipped 0.4% ahead of the open after the index, and the S&P 500 eked out record closing highs.
